en.extract:
The mountain myzomela (Myzomela adolphinae), also known as elfin myzomela or midget myzomela, is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae.
It is found in New Guinea. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.
This species was named in honour of Adolphine Susanna Wilhelmina Bruijn.

The '''mountain myzomela''' ('''''Myzomela adolphinae'''''), also known as '''elfin myzomela''' or '''midget myzomela''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]].
It is found in [[New Guinea]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

This species was named in honour of Adolphine Susanna Wilhelmina Bruijn.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=En4wBAAAQBAJ|title=The Eponym Dictionary of Birds|last1=Beolens|first1=Bo|last2=Watkins|first2=Michael|last3=Grayson|first3=Michael|date=2014-10-01|publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing|isbn=978-1-4729-0574-1|language=en}}</ref>
